name: RLM Workflow
description: Repo-Document Workflow (RLM) with strict phase gates, artifact locking, TDD discipline, and systematic debugging
author: doubleuuser

commands.title: RLM: Implement Requirement
commands.description: Execute RLM workflow for a requirement

commands.title: RLM: Initialize New Run
commands.description: Create a new RLM run with scaffolding

commands.title: RLM: Check Status
commands.description: Check status of current RLM run

rlm.autoResume.description: Automatically resume RLM runs from current phase

skills.name: RLM Workflow
skills.description: Execute RLM (Repo-Document Workflow) end-to-end with strict phase gates, artifact locking, addenda, traceability, and single-command auto-resume behavior
skills.prompt: You are executing the RLM (Repo-Document Workflow) system. Follow the canonical process defined in .agent/PLANS.md. Enforce strict phase gates, artifact locking with SHA-256 hashes, and complete Coverage/Approval gates for each phase. Use TDD discipline (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR) in Phase 4 and systematic debugging (Phase 2b) for bug fixes. Never edit locked prior-phase artifacts; use addenda instead.

skills.name: RLM TDD Discipline
skills.description: Enforce strict RED-GREEN-REFACTOR discipline with The Iron Law - no production code without a failing test first
skills.prompt: You are following TDD discipline for RLM Phase 4. The Iron Law: NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST. Follow strict RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycles: RED (write failing test, verify it fails), GREEN (write minimal code to pass), REFACTOR (clean up while staying green). Document each phase in the TDD Compliance Log. If code was written before test: DELETE IT and start over.

skills.name: RLM Systematic Debugging
skills.description: Systematic 4-phase debugging for root cause analysis before attempting fixes
skills.prompt: You are performing systematic debugging (RLM Phase 2b). The Iron Law: NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST. Follow the 4-phase process: 1) Root Cause Investigation (read errors, reproduce, trace data flow), 2) Pattern Analysis (working vs broken), 3) Hypothesis and Testing (scientific method), 4) Fix Summary. Trace backward from symptom to source. Fix at source, not at symptom.
